ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Apr-08-2026 Continued To May-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Number Two For Order Approving Guardian Ad Litem Fees And Directng Payment By Administrator Jose Alfredo Zermeno, Iii
Off Calendar Feb-13-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James O'Neil for administrator Jose Zermeno III (via Zoom).
Edward Broitman, Guardian ad Litem for minor beneficiary Giovanni Zermeno

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Final Distribution and Petition for Order Approving Guardian Ad Litem Fees and Directing Payment by the Administrator.

The Court noted issues raised by the probate examiner remained outstanding.

The Court ordered the petition for final distribution taken off calendar and directed that a new petition be filed correcting the identified deficiencies. The Court set a new hearing for Status on Close of Estate for April 8, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

With respect to the guardian ad litem's petition for fees, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to fix the final fees and costs but to reserve jurisdiction to determine the source of payment pending resolution of issues to be addressed in the new petition.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Dec-23-2024 Continued To Jun-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James O'Neil for Jose Zermeno III, Administrator (via Zoom).

Mr. O'Neil reports that tax returns are still being worked on. The Franchise Tax Board recently filed a creditor's claim for approximately $6,000. Jewelry allegedly belonging to the decedent is being held by the mother and uncle of the minor beneficiary at a separate property and information regarding the jewelry is still being gathered. An interim accounting is planned prior to filing a petition for final distribution.

The court continued the status hearing to June 30, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar Jan-11-2022 Continued To Feb-08-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Neil, via ZOOM, for Alfredo Zermeno III, Administrator; Lizeth Canales, in court, in propria persona, Interested Party.

The court notes that Mr. Zermeno, III, did not make an appearance this morning. Mr. O'Neil reports that he advised his client that he would appear on his behalf and apologizes to the court for the misunderstanding. The court gently reminds Mr. O'Neil that when the court issues an Order to Appear, it expects all parties that are ordered to make an appearance, should do so. The court then turns to Ms. Canales and asks for a status of an appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem. Ms. Canales is not familiar with the court's request. The court summarizes what the obligations of a Guardian Ad Litem in this matter would be. Mr. O'Neil agrees to assist Ms. Canales with the preceding. The court and Mr. O'Neil discuss the position of this case. Mr. O'Neil advises that there is a related claim pending which is set for trial in March and that he has a status petition which is ready to file in this matter.

After additional discussion, the Order to Appear is taken off calendar. The Status on Close of Estate and Status of Appointment of Guardian Ad Litem are continued to Feb-08-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Retain Litigation Counsel And For Approval Of Attorney-Client Fee Agreements
Off Calendar Aug-11-2020 Advanced From Oct-05-20 To Sep-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Authority To Retain Litigation Counsel And For Approval Of Attorney-Client Fee Agreements
Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; James Lamping appeared by zoom for Jose Zermeno III (Personal Representative).

The Court questions whether Mr. Lamping is a litigation Attorney. Mr. Lamping stated that he does some litigation, that the issues surrounding this case involves unlawful detainer matters, that he does not do unlawful detainers and that one of the persons that filed a creditor's claim resides in the property. The Court stated that there is no information to support the $300,000.00 budget with respect to the Wagstaffe Lowenfeldt Firm. Mr. Lamping stated there is no way to know, that if the matter settles in the first day, the fee will be very little and that if it goes to trial, it will be significantly more. The Court raised the issue of Attorneys providing a budget for their Clients and once again stated that there is no support for the $300,000.00 budget. The Court also stated that it is not sure it makes economic sense, that probate fees are governed by statute and that it will not approve a fee agreement at this rate, or any change made to the rate. Mr. Lamping stated that he believes the language includes that fees are to be approved by the Court, that he will petition in the interim and that he believes he is required under the Probate Code to seek authorization from the Court. The Court stated that this is not what was presented and that it will not approve any fee agreement. The Petition for Authority to Retain Litigation Counsel and for Approval of Attorney-Client Fee Agreements is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-06-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Lamping for Jose Zermeno, III (Petitioner).
Gener Benitez for and with Zubeda Rahim (Objector).
Lee Maranto (was representing Andrea Zermeno) also present.
Lizeth Canales (Mother of Minor) also present.

Mr. Lamping provided a proposed order, stated that he has nothing to add to this petition and that he just received an additional pleading yesterday, but he has not had a chance to look at it. Mr. Benitez stated that his Client does not believe that the Petitioner will be biased, raised the issue of the Petitioner's conviction and stated that there was no influence by Ms. Rahim as to the other objectors. The Court spoke of a fiduciary's responsibility to the heirs and stated that it will be overseen by the Court. Mr. Maranto stated that without authority, Mr. Lamping contacted Andrea, that she initially objected to the petition and is now for it, and that he is here to withdraw. Mr. Lamping clarified that he had no direct contact with Andrea, that his Client talked to his Sister and that all four of the Decedent's adult Children agree to his Client being appointed. The Court informed Mr. Maranto that he needs to have his Client file a substitution. Ms. Canales stated that she submitted a request for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Lizette Canales (Mother of Gioviani Zermeno) in Propria Persona.

The objections are to be filed by August 21, 2019. Responses are to be filed by August 28, 2019.
